Of the Usurpers Magnentius and Vetranio.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Of the Usurpers Magnentius and Vetranio.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: About this time an extraordinary commotion shook the whole state, of the principal heads, of which we shall give a brief account, deeming it necessary not to pass over them altogether.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: We mentioned in our first book, that after the death of the founder of Constantinople, his three sons succeeded him in the empire: it must now be also stated, that a kinsman of theirs, Dalmatius, so named from his father, shared with them the imperial authority.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This person after being associated with them in the sovereignty for a very little while, the soldiers put to death, Constantius having neither commanded his destruction, nor forbidden it.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The manner in which Constantine the younger was also killed by the soldiers, on his invading that division of the empire which belonged to his brother, has already been recorded more than once.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: After his death, the Persian war was raised against the Romans, in which Constantius did nothing prosperously: for in a battle fought by night on the frontiers of both parties, the Persians had to some slight extent the advantage.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And this at a time when the affairs of the Christians became no less unsettled, there being great disturbance throughout the churches on account of Athanasius, and the term homoousion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Affairs having reached this pass, there sprang up a tyrant in the western parts called Magnentius, who by treachery slew Constans, the emperor of the western division of the empire, at that time residing in the Gauls.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This being done, a furious civil war arose, and Magnentius made himself master of all Italy, reduced Africa and Libya under his power, and even obtained possession of the Gauls.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But at the city of Sirmium in Illyricum, the military set up another tyrant whose name was Vetranio; while a fresh trouble threw Rome itself into commotion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: For there was a nephew of Constantine’s, Nepotian by name, who, supported by a body of gladiators, there assumed the sovereignty.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He was, however, slain by some of the officers of Magnentius, who himself invaded the western provinces, and spread desolation in every direction.
